channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Initial results of imprecise census in Myanmar show a slight population drop
item.description: Myanmar’s military government has announced that provisional results of a census taken in October put the Southeast Asian nation’s population at about 51.3 million, a slight drop from the last official nationwide count of about 51.5 million taken 10 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea to inspect Boeing aircraft as it struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines, as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine: How the war shifted in 2024
item.description: A third year of war in Ukraine drags on as the new year and the conflict's February anniversary approach. The war in 2024 saw major shifts on the battlefield and in Western politics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean court issues warrants to detain impeached President Yoon and search his office
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court issued warrants Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol and search his office and residence over allegations of rebellion in connection with his short-lived declaration of martial law.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US and Boeing investigators examine the site of a deadly South Korean plane crash
item.description: A team of U.S. investigators including representatives from Boeing have examined the site of a plane crash that killed 179 people in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Dozens of patients and wounded evacuated from Gaza for treatment
item.description: Dozens of patients and the wounded have been evacuated for treatment outside the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, where the United Nations says Israel’s attacks on and around hospitals have pushed health care to the brink.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Zealand's Auckland is the first major city to welcome 2025 with fireworks and light show
item.description: Auckland has become the first major city to welcome 2025, with thousands counting down to the new year and cheering at fireworks launched from New Zealand’s tallest structure, Sky Tower.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Politician among protesters arrested in Kenya while calling for an end to alleged abductions
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — An opposition politician and several other protesters were arrested in Kenya on Monday during street demonstrations calling for an end to alleged abductions, that recently targeted young government critics.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel intercepts Houthi missile fired from Yemen
item.description: Israel’s military said it intercepted a missile fired toward the country by Yemen’s Houthi rebels, setting off sirens late Monday in central areas including Tel Aviv.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court issues arrest warrant for South Korea's President Yoon
item.description: A South Korean court issues arrest warrant for sitting President Yoon Suk Yeol over his decision in December to impose martial law over the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean court issues warrants to detain impeached President Yoon and search his office
item.description: A South Korean court issued warrants Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol and search his office and residence over his short-lived declaration of martial law.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: National Guard soldier dies days after Christmas in 'non-combat' related incident in Iraq
item.description: An Indiana National Guard soldier deployed to Iraq in support of Operation Inherent Resolve died Saturday in a non-combat incident, Defense Department officials announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin offers to pay off debts as recruitment tool in war against Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a bill into law last month that offers to pay off up to $96,000 in debt in exchange for military service.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about H-1B visas at the center of Musk-MAGA fight
item.description: A simmering debate over work visas exploded over the last days of the year, exposing a rift over immigration among President-elect Trump’s closest supporters.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Hundreds of soldiers freed in the latest prisoner exchange between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine have exchanged prisoners of war in the latest such swap that saw the release of hundreds of captives and was brokered with the help of the United Arab Emirates, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Germany accuses Musk of trying to sway election
item.description: The German government on Monday accused Elon Musk of trying to influence its February election after the world’s wealthiest person penned an op-ed this weekend backing the far-right political party Alternative for Germany (AfD).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis kicks off a yearlong Jubilee that will test his stamina and Rome's patience
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis kicked off the 2025 Holy Year on Tuesday, inaugurating a celebration of the Catholic Church that is expected to draw some 32 million pilgrims to Rome in a test of the pope's stamina and the ability of the Eternal City to welcome them.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli spy network uncovers Hezbollah commander's plans to marry his 4 mistresses
item.description: Israeli spy agencies infiltrated Hezbollah so successfully that they learned intimate details of commander Fuad Shukr's plans to marry his four mistresses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US sends Ukraine final Biden-approved weapons, financial package
item.description: The Biden administration's final aid to Ukraine before Donald Trump takes office is a $1.25 billion drawdown package.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: US sends Ukraine final Biden-approved weapons, financial package
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday its final weapons and aid package to Ukraine before President-elect Trump is set to take the reins in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Finnish investigators find an anchor drag mark on the Baltic seabed after suspicious cable damage
item.description: HELSINKI, Finland (AP) — Finnish investigators probing the damage to a Baltic Sea power cable and several data cables said they found an anchor drag mark on the seabed, apparently from a Russia-linked vessel that has already been seized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbian court convicts the parents of a teenage boy who shot dead 10 people in a school in Belgrade
item.description: A court in Serbia has convicted and sentenced to prison terms the parents of a teenage boy who shot dead nine pupils and a school guard last year and wounded six more people in primary school in central Belgrade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Syria rebel leader says new elections could take up to 4 years
item.description: Ahmad al-Sharaa, the leader of the new governing authority in Syria, said in a Sunday interview that it could take up to four years to hold elections in the country.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: South Korea orders emergency safety probe of airline operations after plane crash kills 179
item.description: South Korean acting President Choi Sang-mok ordered an emergency safety inspection of the country’s entire airline operation on Monday, a day after a fiery Jeju Air crash killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: South Korea struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kavelashvili is inaugurated as Georgia's president, complicating its path toward EU
item.description: TBILISI, Goergia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili was formally inaugurated as president of Georgia on Sunday, cementing the ruling party's grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Gaza's crowded tent camps, women wrestle with a life stripped of privacy
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza City (AP) — For Gaza’s women, the hardships of life in the territory’s sprawling tent camps are compounded by the daily humiliation of never having privacy.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean families pray for relatives who vanished into North Korean prisons a decade ago
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In the past year, Choi Jin-young has done everything he can to bring attention to his father, a missionary who's serving a life sentence in North Korea. He’s met senior U.N., U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Flights temporarily suspended at Halifax airport after airplane landing incident
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia (AP) — The Transportation Safety Board of Canada is investigating an incident involving a plane at the Halifax Stanfield International Airport, which one passenger described as a rough landing that sparked flames.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden 'deeply saddened' by Jeju Air accident in South Korea
item.description: President Biden said he was “deeply saddened” by the Jeju Air accident in South Korea that left nearly 180 people dead. “Jill and I are deeply saddened to learn of the loss of life that occurred as a result of the Jeju Airlines accident in Muan, Republic of Korea,” Biden said Sunday in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Syria's de facto leader says it could take up to 4 years to hold elections
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syria's de facto leader said Sunday it could take up to four years to hold elections in Syria, and that he plans to dissolve his Islamist group that led the country's insurgency at an anticipated national dialogue summit for the country.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China unveils world's largest amphibious warship
item.description: China has launched a new amphibious assault ship, named the Sichuan, which is capable of launching fighter jets and is designed to compete with U.S. military strength in the seas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
